title: cytisus leucanthus subsp. obscurus
intro: Cytisus leucanthus subsp. obscurus, also known as the white broom, is a shrub plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and providing antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Cytisus leucanthus subsp. obscurus can be made by infusing the dried flowers in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
